hero.lede: A commercial office in the heart of Helsinki. High ceilings, hard surfaces, and a reverberation problem that made the space feel louder than it was.

brief.body_p1: The space had a measured reverberation time (RT60) above 1.2 seconds — typical for an untreated office with high ceilings and exposed concrete. Conversations blurred. Meetings felt tiring.
brief.body_p2: The challenge was treating the ceiling without drop-in suspended panels or visible absorption baffles. The brief called for a flush, neutral ceiling surface that could be specified directly from an architectural drawing.
brief.body_p3: Aisti® at 40 mm, mounted with a 200 mm batten to create an air gap, was the specification. The wider tile format — 600×1200 mm — reduced the number of visible joints and kept the ceiling plane clean.

results.note: Reverberation time measured in-situ pre and post installation. Absorption coefficients from EN ISO 354 reverberation room test. Configuration: 40 mm / 200 mm air gap.

cta.lede: Send the brief. We'll help you select format, thickness, and mounting for your room — and get you samples ahead of full production in August 2026.
